scope: rocprofv3 kernel trace for the Actual-LI S4096 layer-2 dense local compressed-group dKV HIP diagnostic on one MI350.

probe_event_timing.read: Use trace_kernel_stats.csv for the profiled kernel duration. The in-probe CUDA event timing is included only as a sanity check.

kernel_static_trace_row.grid_size_read: rocprof reports total work-items for this HIP dispatch; the logical CTA grid is seq_tiles * selected_tiles * d_blocks = 128 * 2 * 32 = 8192.
kernel_static_trace_row.lds_block_size_read: rocprof did not report the dynamic shared-memory allocation; use dense_group_dynamic_shared_bytes_from_probe.

write_accounting.read: This profile uses the lower-pressure selected_block=256 bring-up rung, so it gets half of the primary selected_block=512 write-reduction target.

decision_read.what_this_proves: The dense local compressed-group table HIP kernel itself is the measured wall, not Python overhead, memory copies, or setup kernels.
decision_read.why_rejected: Even after reducing modeled global dKV writes by 11.91x, the scalar per-head accumulation plus local atomic/LDS schedule takes 120.07 ms at S4096.
decision_read.next_kernel_contract: Keep the tile-local reduction idea, but implement it as an MFMA/CK-Tile or equivalent segmented reducer that computes the selected-key dot products in a matrix-shaped loop and reduces dKV locally before global writes.
